_draft_note: INERT STAGING. Lives under godot/data/_drafts/, never loaded by dialogue_runner (the runner uses DirAccess on godot/data/dialogues only). Promote to godot/data/dialogues/tram_waiter_ch1.json once Piotr authors the lines. State ids are globally namespaced (tram_waiter_*) per _schema.md. Tram waiter is a purely transactional street NPC — the transactional carve-out in godot/AGENTS.md §First-meeting introductions applies, so no name-exchange first-meeting beat is required. He stands at the tram stop on office_street.tscn (Tram17 platform) and reacts to the player approaching the rails. Address form toward Cula: 'Dr. A. Cula' if he speaks to him by name, but the more idiomatic register here is muttering to the platform, not direct address.
_chapter_ref: Chapter 1 ambient. Beat 1 (arrival walk to the office) and reusable thereafter. No quest-critical content; this NPC exists to make Office Street feel inhabited.
_address_form_note: Toward Cula: 'Dr. A. Cula' if direct address is used. Polish service register is in scope (tram-platform mutter) but per godot/AGENTS.md §Stack invariants the English-first rule applies; keep institutional terms in English where possible (e.g. 'the 17 is delayed' not 'siedemnastka spóźniona') unless Piotr decides a tram-stop ambient line earns a Polish phrase per English-first carve-outs.
_authoring_todo: Replace every line beginning with '_doc:' with the finished Taste-Standard line.
Decide whether tram_waiter_first_approach should chain into a dwell option (tram-arrival timer joke) or stay one-shot.
Decide whether the waiter gets a delayed-tram reaction state gated on a future tram_arrived flag (currently unwired; out of scope for the skeleton).

lines: _doc: DRAFT — one line. Waiter mutters about the 17 being late, addressed to the platform, not the player. Keep institutional/transit terms English-first.
_doc: DRAFT — one line, repeatable. Sibling shape to first_approach. A second observation about the delay or the weather; nothing that advances quest state.

idle_flavor: _doc: DRAFT — one short idle line played at random if no state trigger matches. Optional; can be dropped.
